dash.subtitle: Scheduled PDF / Excel reports, emailed to recipients
dash.emailOff: Email delivery is OFF — reports are generated and archived but not sent. Enable it in Settings.
dash.none: No runs yet. Open a report and choose Run now.
rep.title: Reports
rep.subtitle: Define what to generate, in which formats, and with which engine
rep.none: No reports yet. Create one to get started.
det.noSchedules: No schedules. Add one and click Sync schedules to activate.
det.noRecipients: No recipients. Add one to email this report.
runs.title: Run History
runs.subtitle: Every generation attempt, its outputs and delivery status
rd.title: Run
rd.noDeliveries: No deliveries (email off or no recipients).
set.title: Settings
set.subtitle: Runtime and email (SMTP) configuration for the Python engine
set.note: These settings are read by the report runner each cycle. The SMTP password is NOT stored here — it stays in the runner's env.ps1 / secret store.
set.secret: Secret — managed in env.ps1, not editable here
set.testHint: Send a test message via the database mail (APEX_MAIL) to confirm outbound email works. Uses the sender in SMTP_FROM; delivery also needs the APEX instance SMTP settings configured.
col.name: Name
f.subject: Email subject template
det.runParamsHint: Fill the run parameters (leave a field empty to use the report default). Numbers are sent as numbers.
det.psTitle: Run Parameters — labels, hints and lists of values
det.psHint: Per-parameter UI metadata used by the Run drawer and the Interactive Report viewer. The LOV query runs as ADMIN — reference PROD objects with the prod. prefix; first column = value, optional second column = display label.
det.psNone: This report declares no run parameters (add defaults in the definition's params JSON first).
det.psTestFail: Query failed — must be a bind-free SELECT
wrk.title: Workers
wrk.subtitle: Report engine workers — live health, queue backlog and in-database jobs
wrk.backlog: {0} run(s) waiting in the queue — the oldest for {1} minute(s). Check that a worker is online, or requeue stuck runs.
wrk.none: No workers have registered yet. Start one with: python runner.py --forever
wrk.nativeDesc: Sweeps NATIVE-engine queued runs every 5 minutes (in-DB PDF/Excel)
wrk.maintDesc: Reclaims stuck runs + purges expired outputs every 15 minutes
dash.userHeroSub: Run a report, then filter, sort, hide columns, add calculations and export to Excel.
ir.viewer.title: Interactive Reports
ir.viewer.subtitle: Run a report and work with its data in the browser
ir.viewer.hint: Set the parameters and click Run to load the data.
